original_text:
### Layout Principles
- Generous white space; clean, never cluttered. **Limit focal points per section** — use contrast to direct attention to the single most important element.
- "Show, don't tell": break up text walls with imagery and icons.
- Color blocks delineate topics (My Compass webpage pattern) — alternate White / Light Blue / Golden Sand / gradient section backgrounds so adjacent assembled sections don't visually merge. **[GENERAL]: section designers must declare their section's background color in the component metadata so assemblers can sequence without two identical adjacent backgrounds.**
- Bold headline + simple benefit statements + patient quote is the canonical content block pattern (per patient brochure, which is the **reference template for visuals, messaging, and overall tone of ALL LISRAYA materials**).

quotes: Limit focal points per section — use contrast to direct attention to the single most important element.
Generous white space; clean, never cluttered. **Limit focal points per section** — use contrast to direct attention to the single most important element.

rules.intent: Maintain clean layouts with clear visual hierarchy and avoid visual clutter.
rules.rule_text: Each layout section must limit focal points to direct attention to the single most important element.

quotes: Color blocks delineate topics (My Compass webpage pattern) — alternate White / Light Blue / Golden Sand / gradient section backgrounds so adjacent assembled sections don't visually merge. **[GENERAL]: section designers must declare their section's background color in the component metadata so assemblers can sequence without two identical adjacent backgrounds.**

rules.intent: Delineate topics clearly by preventing adjacent identical backgrounds from blending together.
rules.rule_text: Adjacent sections must not use the same background color. Section designers must declare backgrounds in metadata to allow assemblers to sequence alternate colors (White, Light Blue, Golden Sand, or gradients) and prevent sections from visually merging.
